commit.message:
EXP-1481-1490: Clinical translation & ADA guideline validation

10 experiments translating pipeline to clinical practice:
- EXP-1481: 9/11 insulin-sensitive phenotype (AID amplifies)
- EXP-1482: Dose-response R²<0.08 — AID confounds ISF calibration
- EXP-1483: ISF circadian amplitude 12%, sinusoidal fit poor
- EXP-1484: Personalized targets inflate TIR, keep standard 70-180
- EXP-1485: 8/11 overcount carbs (systematic bias)
- EXP-1486: AID target range 64-149 mg/dL (huge config variation)
- EXP-1487: Natural experiments best for CR, worst for ISF
- EXP-1488: Long-term projections unreliable beyond 3 months
- EXP-1489: Clinical reports average 117 words (workflow-ready)
- EXP-1490: Pipeline-ADA alignment only 64% — TBR not tracked

CRITICAL: Pipeline needs TBR (time-below-range) safety check.
5/11 patients exceed ADA 4% TBR threshold undetected.
Pipeline v10 proposal: add TBR to grading formula (15% weight).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(emitter): cover False branch of config_candidates check in emit_extension_files

When an extension artifact has raw_content set, emit_extension_files uses
find_map to locate which file in artifact.files is the config source.  The
find_map closure evaluates each file name against config_candidates.  All
existing tests ordered the config file first in the files vec, so the closure
always returned Some immediately and the False branch (non-config filename)
was never reached.

Add emit_extension_files_non_config_before_config_covers_false_branch which
places index.js before config.json in artifact.files.  The find_map now
evaluates index.js first (False — not a config candidate) before finding
config.json (True), covering the previously-missed branch.

Branch coverage: emitter.rs 92.79% → 93.27%; overall 92.22% → 92.27%.

  UNIX_LINES up by one bit position)

  and inside character classes when COMMENTS is active

  parsePerlFlags()
- Add comprehensive CommentsTest covering: basic whitespace/comment
  stripping, character class behavior, inline flag toggling, flag
  combinations, and edge cases (empty patterns, \Q...\E quoting,
  LITERAL interaction)

Fix create-failing-test-issue: remove --no-restore, add workflow_dispatch (#15973)

The restore.sh step restores projects defined in eng/Build.props
(src/, tests/, playground/, eng/) but not tools/. The --no-restore
flag on dotnet build prevents the tool project from being restored,
causing NETSDK1004. Remove --no-restore so dotnet build can restore
the tool project itself.

Also add workflow_dispatch trigger so the workflow can be tested
from any branch without needing an issue_comment event on main.
This is useful for validating changes to the workflow itself.

commit.message:
Add path traversal and symlink protection to ExternalTensor (#386)

`ExternalTensor` accepted arbitrary `location` values without verifying
they resolve within `base_dir`, enabling path traversal attacks (e.g.
`location="../../etc/passwd"`). The string-based check alone also could
not detect symbolic links that escape `base_dir`.

## Changes

- **`_core.py`**: All security checks are consolidated in a single
`_check_path_containment()` helper that is called at actual file access
time (`_load()` and `tofile()`). No check is performed at construction.
The helper performs two checks in sequence when `base_dir` is non-empty:
1. **String-based path traversal**: the normalized absolute path must be
contained within `base_dir`. Uses `os.path.normcase` +
`os.path.normpath` + `os.path.abspath` for cross-platform correctness.
Catches `../../etc/passwd`-style escapes (including absolute paths like
`/etc/passwd`) without requiring the file to exist.
2. **Realpath containment**: `os.path.realpath()` is used to fully
resolve all symlinks on both `base_dir` and the file path; the resolved
path must remain within the resolved `base_dir`. Symlinks whose target
resolves within `base_dir` are permitted; symlinks that escape it are
rejected.

No check is performed when `base_dir` is empty (no containment context
available). `tobytes()` is protected indirectly via `_load()` →
`_check_path_containment()`, documented with an auditability comment.

- **`_core_test.py`**: 8 new tests covering direct traversal
(`../../etc/passwd`, rejected at load), traversal through a subdirectory
(`subdir/../../../etc/passwd`, rejected at load), valid subdirectory
path (allowed), empty `base_dir` (no check performed), symlink pointing
outside `base_dir` (rejected at load), symlink pointing within
`base_dir` (allowed at load), path traversal via `tofile()` entry point
(rejected), and absolute location path `/etc/passwd` (rejected at load).

```python
# Construction always succeeds for relative paths
tensor = ExternalTensor("../../etc/passwd", ..., base_dir="/safe/models")

# Raises ValueError at load: resolves outside base_dir
tensor.numpy()

# Raises ValueError at tofile: same check applies
tensor.tofile(some_file)

# Raises ValueError at load: absolute path escapes base_dir
ExternalTensor("/etc/passwd", ..., base_dir="/safe/models").numpy()

# No check at all: base_dir is empty
ExternalTensor("../../anywhere", ..., base_dir="").numpy()  # no error

# Allowed: symlink resolves within base_dir
ExternalTensor("link.bin", ..., base_dir="/safe/models").numpy()  # link.bin -> real.bin inside base_dir

# Raises ValueError at load: symlink escapes base_dir
ExternalTensor("evil_link.bin", ..., base_dir="/safe/models").numpy()  # evil_link.bin -> /etc/passwd
```

---------

Kubernetes RBAC-based authorization via SubjectAccessReview.

- Reuses existing tokenAuthenticator (OIDC-first, TokenReview fallback)
- New authenticateUser() method returns username without SA allowlist
  check, delegating authorization to SAR

Authorization:
- SubjectAccessReview checks get permission on status/dashboard in
  status.net.unbounded-kube.io API group
- Results cached per username (5-minute TTL, 1000 max entries, LRU)
- New unbounded-net-status-viewer ClusterRole with aggregate-to-view
  label, so anyone with view access gets dashboard access

Aggregated API:
- status/json added to API resource discovery
- /apis/status.net.unbounded-kube.io/v1alpha1/status/json endpoint
  always requires front-proxy cert auth (API server handles authz)

kubectl proxy:

- Opens browser to /status by default (--no-browser to skip)

Protected paths: /status, /status/, /status/json, /status/node/*, /status/ws
Unprotected: /healthz, /readyz, /metrics, /static/*, /status/push, /status/nodews

aggregated API. All 17 nodes reporting Healthy.

* feat: HMAC token authentication with node identity enforcement

Replace OIDC/TokenReview auth on push and dashboard endpoints with
controller-issued HMAC tokens validated in O(1) with no API calls.

New token request endpoints (aggregated API):
- POST /apis/.../token/node: validates SA token via TokenReview,
  extracts kubernetes.io.node.name from JWT claims, issues 4-hour
  HMAC token with node claim. Push endpoints enforce node claim
  matches payload -- nodes cannot impersonate other nodes.
- POST /apis/.../token/viewer: issues 30-minute HMAC token for
  dashboard access. Both endpoints perform SAR for authorization.

New pkg/authn package:
- HMAC-SHA256 token generation and validation
- Claims: subject, role (node/viewer), node name, expiry
- Kubernetes SA JWT payload decoder for node name extraction

Controller changes:
- CertManager stores HMAC key in serving cert Secret (persists across
  restarts, preserved during cert rotation)
- Push handlers validate HMAC tokens with role=node + node claim match
- Dashboard handlers validate HMAC tokens with role=viewer
- Token validation cache removed (HMAC is O(1))
- Configurable lifetimes: --node-token-lifetime (4h), --viewer-token-lifetime (30m)

RBAC:
- Node SA: create token/node
- Status viewer: create token/viewer (aggregated to view)
- Controller: keeps tokenreviews + subjectaccessreviews for token endpoint

Note: node agents and kubectl plugin still use old SA tokens and will
get 401 until updated in the next commit.

commit.message:
EXP-1461-1470: Practical implementation & edge cases (190/190)

10 experiments stress-testing pipeline under real-world conditions:
- EXP-1461: Grades stable at 50% data dropout (11/11)
- EXP-1462: 7/11 patients mostly in AID suspend mode
- EXP-1463: Noise tolerance through σ=10 mg/dL (9/11 stable)
- EXP-1464: 4/11 show seasonal patterns, modest effect
- EXP-1465: Biweekly aggregation most stable (default window)
- EXP-1466: CGM artifacts bias TIR by only -0.5%
- EXP-1467: Zero recommendation conflicts (0/11)
- EXP-1468: Dose rounding negligible for 9/11
- EXP-1469: Patient communication summaries ready
- EXP-1470: Integration tests 11/11 pass, <20ms per patient

Pipeline v9 operationally validated for production deployment.

commit.message:
Add 9 Unity scene files with complete interactive UI hierarchies

Build Dashboard, Squad, TransferMarket, Shop, Tactics, Finances,
Competitions, TeamSelection, and Match scenes with full YAML hierarchies
matching MainMenu.unity's format.

Each scene contains:
- Camera + AudioListener, EventSystem (StandaloneInputModule)
- Canvas root (CanvasScaler 1080×1920, GraphicRaycaster)
- UI script MonoBehaviour with all SerializeField refs wired to
  the correct component fileIDs (Button, TMP, Dropdown, Slider, InputField)
- Interactive panels, navigation buttons, hidden overlay panels
- No duplicate fileIDs within each scene

Also add backButton SerializeField + OnBack() navigation to:
- FinancesUI.cs (added using UnityEngine.UI, Awake, OnBack)
- SquadUI.cs (added backButton wiring in Awake, OnBack)
- TacticsUI.cs (added backButton field, wired in Start, OnBack)
- CompetitionsUI.cs (added backButton field, wired in Start, OnBack)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Alterms77 <262047946+Alterms77@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(frontend): overhaul chat UI/UX inspired by ChatGPT/Gemini

- Add CodeBlock component with syntax highlighting, language label, copy button
- Add ThinkingIndicator with animated avatar, bouncing dots, elapsed timer
- Redesign MessageBubble: clean prose for assistant, violet pill for user,
  hover copy/retry actions, full markdown (tables, blockquotes, GFM)
- Redesign ReasoningPanel with CSS variables, framer-motion staggered tools
- Redesign ChatInput with unified send/stop button, AnimatePresence, focus ring
- Wire isThinking/thinkingStartTime state through useChatState and ChatContainer
- Handle thinking events in useStreamingChat with proper cleanup
- Add streaming cursor, thinking shimmer, prose typography, syntax theme to CSS
- Soften message entrance animation in animations.ts
- Add remark-gfm, rehype-highlight, highlight.js dependencies

refactor: extract shared server logic; add checkPlatform helper

  AskWithTimeout and AskWithHTML
- Add startDialogServer(mux) eliminating duplicated net.Listen, srv.Serve,
  openBrowser, and graceful Shutdown logic (~20 lines removed)
